free_text: Differential cross sections of neutron scattering on
elemental silicon at energies between 7.89 MeV
(1 ns pulse width) and multi-angle neutron
Deuterium gas target, 1.0 cm in diameter and
3.0 cm in length, gas pressure 1.9-2.0 hPa.
Mean neutron energy is defined within +/- 20 keV.
n/gamma discrimination with pulse shape analysis.
Five NE213 neutron detectors located behind a
massive collimator shielding in a distance of
1200 cm from the sample. The angle separation
The angle region covered is 12.5 deg to 160 deg.
An additional NE213 detector was used as relative
fluence monitor, diameter 3.81 cm, length 3.81 cm.
The movable monitor (in a fixed distance of 651 cm)
was always in a position of 58.9 deg relative
diameter 25.0 mm, height 50.0 mm, mass 57.069 g,
placed 17.5 cm from the gas target centre.
Monte Carlo simulated TOF spectra of the complete
angular distribution were iteratively adjusted to
the measured TOF spectra. The simulation comprised
the neutron production in the gas target and the
scattering geometry between target, sample and
Total errors comprise counting statistics and
Error due to   angle uncert. (0.2 deg),
Error due to multiple scattering correct. (5%).
contain an additional uncertainty component of
between 1.2% and 17% in order to consider the
influence of the chosen maximum order L-max
of the fitted Legendre polynomial on the integral
Inelastic cross sections were extracted based on
the assumption of an isotopic abundance of Si-28
SD: Ref. on Conf. abstract deleted
according to Memo CP-D/791. Minor corrections.
SD: ERR-2 -> ANG-ERR in COMMON section.
